text: Progressive overload: increase one variable per week (load, reps, sets, density) by ~5-10%. Do not raise weekly volume by more than ~10% to limit injury risk.
Acute:chronic workload ratio (ACWR) sweet spot is 0.8-1.3. Above 1.5 sharply increases soft-tissue injury risk in team-sport athletes.
source: Gabbett (2016), British Journal of Sports Medicine

text: Rate of Perceived Exertion (RPE) on a 1-10 scale: RPE 6 = 4 reps in reserve, RPE 8 = 2 RIR, RPE 9 = 1 RIR, RPE 10 = max effort. Use RPE 6-8 for hypertrophy/strength accumulation, 8-9 for intensification.
Autoregulation via RPE allows day-to-day adjustment when fatigue is high (low HRV, poor sleep). Reduce target load by 5-10% to maintain target RPE without overshooting fatigue.
source: Zourdos et al., Strength & Conditioning Journal

text: Deload weeks every 4-6 weeks reduce volume to ~50-60% with similar intensity. They preserve strength, restore CNS, and allow soft-tissue recovery.
Signs to trigger an unscheduled deload: 3+ consecutive sessions with stalled performance, persistent soreness >72h, HRV drop >10% from rolling baseline, sleep <6h for 3+ nights.
Hypertrophy: 10-20 hard sets per muscle group per week (RPE 7-9). Beginners closer to 10, advanced lifters up to 20 with proper recovery.
Strength: 4-6 hard sets per main lift per week at 80-90% 1RM, 3-5 reps, 3-5 min rest. Doublets and singles included near peak.
source: NSCA Essentials of Strength Training & Conditioning, 4e

text: Power and explosive output: 1-3 reps per set at 30-50% of 1RM with maximal bar speed. 3-6 sets, full recovery (2-5 min) between sets to maintain quality.
Training a muscle group 2x/week produces ~3.1% greater hypertrophy than 1x/week with matched volume. Spreading volume preserves quality and reduces fatigue per session.
Rest 3-5 minutes between sets for compound strength/power lifts; 60-120 s for hypertrophy accessories; 30-60 s for metabolic finishers.
Warm-up: 5-8 min general (raise core temp), then RAMP (Raise, Activate, Mobilise, Potentiate). Add 2-3 ramp-up sets at 50-70-85% before working sets.
Nordic hamstring exercise 2x/week reduces hamstring strain incidence by ~51% in soccer players. Start with 2x5 eccentric reps, progress to 3x8-12 over 6-8 weeks.
source: Petersen et al. (2011); van Dyk meta-analysis (2019)

text: Hamstring training balance: combine hip-dominant (RDL, Nordic) and knee-flexion (leg curl) for full posterior chain coverage. Eccentric-biased work most protective.
source: Bourne et al., Hamstring strain prevention

text: Goalkeepers prioritize lateral power, reactive jumps, and rotational core strength. Heavy lower-body strength 2x/week, plyometrics emphasizing lateral bound and box jumps.
Centre-backs need maximal strength, aerial power, and short-distance acceleration (5-15 m). Squat/deadlift base + jump squats + sled pushes.
source: Position-specific demands — Di Salvo et al.

text: Fullbacks/wingbacks cover 11-12 km/match with high repeated-sprint demand. Train repeated-sprint ability (6x40 m, 20 s rest) plus eccentric hamstring work.
source: Repeated Sprint Ability — Bishop et al.

text: Central midfielders need aerobic power (vVO2max work) and repeated change-of-direction. 4 min @ 90-95% HRmax x 4 reps with 3 min jog. Add COD ladders 2x/week.
Wingers train top-end speed (30-40 m sprints), 1v1 deceleration, and unilateral power (split squat jumps). 6-8 max sprints with 90-180 s rest.
Strikers prioritize first-step quickness, jump height, and shot power. Trap-bar jumps, depth jumps from 30-40 cm, and rotational med-ball throws.
High-intensity intervals (HIIT) 2x/week improve VO2max more than steady state. 4x4 min at 90-95% HRmax with 3 min active recovery is the gold standard.
Sport-specific conditioning: small-sided games (4v4 to 6v6) on reduced pitch elicit HR responses similar to interval running while preserving technical work.
Maximal aerobic speed (MAS) intervals: 15 s @ 100-120% MAS / 15 s rest x 12-20 reps. Builds repeated-sprint capacity without excessive volume.
Plyometric progression: extensive (low-intensity, high-volume hops) → intensive (depth jumps, single-leg) → reactive. 4-6 weeks per phase, 60-120 ground contacts/session.
Drop jump height 30-50 cm optimizes reactive strength index (RSI) for most athletes. Land on mid-foot, keep ground contact <250 ms.
Maximum-velocity sprinting requires full recovery: 1 min per 10 m sprinted, 6-10 sprints/session, 1-2 sessions/week. Keep total volume <300 m sprinted.
Acceleration training (10-20 m): resisted sprints with 10-20% body-mass sled load improve early acceleration mechanics. 6-8 reps with full recovery.
Core training: prioritize anti-extension (planks, dead bug), anti-rotation (pallof press), and anti-lateral flexion (suitcase carry). 2-3 exercises x 2-3 sets, 2-3x/week.
Sleep 7-9 h/night is the single largest recovery lever. Each hour below 7 increases injury risk ~1.7x in adolescent athletes.
HRV (RMSSD) tracked daily: a >10% drop from 7-day rolling average for 2+ days suggests reduced readiness. Lower intensity or convert to skill/recovery.
Protein intake 1.6-2.2 g/kg/day distributed across 3-5 meals supports hypertrophy and recovery. Post-workout 0.3-0.4 g/kg within 2 h optimal.
Active recovery (15-30 min low-intensity cycling/walking) the day after high-intensity work clears metabolites and reduces DOMS more than complete rest.
Beginners (<1 year training): 3 full-body sessions/week with compound lifts (squat, hinge, push, pull, carry). 2-3 sets x 8-12 reps, RPE 6-7.
Intermediate (1-3 years): upper/lower split 4x/week or push/pull/legs 6x/week. Include accessory volume + autoregulated top sets.
Advanced (3+ years): block periodization (accumulation 3-4 wk → intensification 2-3 wk → realization 1-2 wk → deload). Specialize one quality per block.
Fat loss: target ~0.5-1% body weight loss per week via -300 to -500 kcal deficit. Maintain protein 2.0-2.4 g/kg and 2x/week resistance training to preserve lean mass.
source: Helms et al., natural physique research

text: Endurance base: 80% of weekly volume at conversational pace (Z2, 60-75% HRmax), 20% at threshold/HIIT. Polarized model outperforms middle-intensity grind.
FIFA 11+ warm-up done 2x/week reduces overall injury rate ~30% and ACL injuries ~50% in football. 20 minutes pre-training.
Change-of-direction (COD) training: 5-0-5 test, T-test drills, and reactive cuts off a coach signal. 4-6 reps, 60-90 s rest, 2x/week, surrounded by lower-body strength.
Deceleration capacity (eccentric quad strength) is highly correlated with COD performance and injury resilience. Single-leg squats and slow lowering 4-0-X tempo.
source: Harper et al., deceleration in football

text: Mobility focus before training: hip flexor and ankle dorsiflexion CARs, T-spine rotation. 5-8 minutes; long static holds (>60 s) only post-session or on rest days.
Lumbar spine pain: avoid loaded spinal flexion under fatigue. Substitute back squat with safety-bar or front squat, RDL with hip thrust if pain persists.
Knee pain (anterior/patellar): avoid deep BB squats temporarily; use leg press at 60-90° and add Spanish squats and slow eccentric step-downs to load tendon.
Shoulder discomfort: replace overhead press with landmine press or incline DB press; prioritize external rotators (face pulls, band pull-aparts) and serratus work.
1-day/week minimum effective dose: full-body, 3 compound lifts (squat, hinge, push or pull), 2-3 sets at RPE 7-8. Maintains strength, slight gains in beginners.
2-3 days/week: full-body or upper/lower (2 days) / push-pull-legs (3 days). Compound lifts every session, 4-6 working sets per body part across the week.
4-6 days/week: upper/lower x2 or push/pull/legs x2. Allow 48 h between same-muscle high-intensity sessions. Watch total volume — fatigue compounds.
If user picks 'Auto' for days/week: choose 4 d/wk for general fitness, 5 d/wk for sport-specific or hypertrophy goals, 3 d/wk for true beginners or fat-loss with cardio.
If user picks 'Auto' for plan length: 6 weeks for fat-loss/general fitness, 8 weeks for sport-specific/strength, 12 weeks only when timing a competition or peak.
Session-length cap: under 45 min, prioritize 1 main lift + 2 accessories + 5 min finisher. Under 30 min, choose density circuits (EMOM/AMRAP) over straight sets.
